Narrative: CT SINUS / FACIAL WITHOUT CONTRAST EXAM DATE: 9/15/2025 2:34 PM. COMPARISON: None
INDICATION: Punch in the face two weeks ago and has had difficulty breathing through her nose due to the
swelling. TECHNIQUE: 1.25 mm Axial CT images of the face were obtained in bone and soft tissue algorithm.
Coronal and sagittal reformatted images were also examined. DOSE REPORT: This study involved (1) CT
acquisition(s). The CTDIvol and DLP values are included below as required by state law: 1; Series: 4; Head16 cm;
CTDIvol: 25 mGy; DLP: 600 mGy-cm For further information on CT radiation dose, see
http://www.ucdmc.ucdavis.edu/radiology/RadiationDose.html FINDINGS: Facial fractures: Fracture of the right
nasal bone without significant
displacement. Orbits: No evidence of acute orbital fracture. No evidence of vitreous or post septal hemorrhage.
Paranasal Sinuses and mastoid air cells: Partial opacification of the anterior ethmoid air cells. Soft tissues: No
significant subcutaneous edema. Visualized brain parenchyma: No significant abnormality is noted. IMPRESSION:
1. Fracture of the right nasal bone without significant displacement, which may be acute or chronic in nature. 2.
Otherwise, no evidence of acute facial fracture.
I have personally reviewed the images of this study and agree
with the above report. Final Report Electronically Signed By: Matthew Bobinski, M.D. OFF

Certified Interpreter Utilized: No interpreter
Visit Mode: In Person
Subjective
Nicole is a 35 year old female here today for Referral (To ent, ) and Appointment (Med refill acyclovir,
losartan & nicotine patches)
The patient is here today for a follow-up visit to request several medications and some testing. Patient needs
medication refills. Also is due for updated screening labs. Requesting STD testing. Her recent male sexual
partner tested positive for syphilis. She did not know at the time of intercourse. Condoms were not used.
Currently she has recurrent genital herpes. She needs refills on her acyclovir. She is alert and oriented.
Appears fatigued.
Appears older than stated age. Patient also has a nasal fracture which occurred 2 months ago. She believes
that it did not assault. Her stepfather has come to her home and share her face. She was seen in the ER and
police report was filed. She missed her initial follow-up visit with PCP. She was able to get a visit today and is
requesting an urgent referral to ENT for her nasal fracture. Sinus CT scan completed 2 months ago confirms
diagnosis.
Vital signs show elevated BP today. Other vitals are within normal range. She appears fatigued and
uncomfortable.
Please note: Patient was advised to keep a organize list of concerns for each appointment. Also needs to
have an organized digital or paper file for her health issues.
Review of Systems
Constitutional:
See HPI.

claude_vision.referral_reason: Urgent ENT consultation for closed fracture of the right nasal bone with delayed healing following blunt trauma (assault by stepfather). Patient is a 35-year-old female with a nasal fracture that occurred approximately 2 months prior to the referral date of 12/12/2025. The referring provider notes the patient is 'well past the window for closed reduction' and the referral is marked Urgent [2].
relevant_clinical_findings: 35-year-old female with nasal fracture resulting from blunt trauma (assault — stepfather struck her face)
Injury occurred approximately 2 months before referral (circa early-mid October 2025)
Patient was seen in the ER at time of injury; police report was filed
Missed initial PCP follow-up after ER visit
Chief complaint includes difficulty breathing through nose due to swelling at time of CT
Physical exam on 12/12/2025: Nose shows congestion and rhinorrhea present
Triage note from PA Goldfarb/Lee Caroline states: 'Well past window for closed reduction. Not urgent' — however referring MD marked as Urgent [2]
No hearing test needed per scheduling note on 12/19/2025
BP elevated at 150/95 on day of visit (not directly ENT-relevant but noted as context)
Patient appears fatigued and uncomfortable on exam
claude_vision.imaging_summary: CT SINUS/FACIAL WITHOUT CONTRAST performed 09/15/2025. FINDINGS: Fracture of the right nasal bone without significant displacement. Orbits: No evidence of acute orbital fracture. No evidence of vitreous or post-septal hemorrhage. Paranasal sinuses and mastoid air cells: Partial opacification of the anterior ethmoid air cells. Soft tissues: No significant subcutaneous edema. Visualized brain parenchyma: No significant abnormality noted. IMPRESSION: (1) Fracture of the right nasal bone without significant displacement, which may be acute or chronic in nature. (2) Otherwise, no evidence of acute facial fracture. Signed by Matthew Bobinski, M.D. CT report IS included in the faxed documentation (pages 8 of 15).
missing_information: No formal ENT-specific clinical notes from the referring provider beyond the PCP progress note — no otolaryngology pre-assessment
Authorization document references 'Vivant auth' attached but the complete insurance authorization details are partially redacted/illegible in the fax
Initial ER visit records not included (ER evaluation at time of injury not attached)
Police report referenced but not included
No photos or external nasal exam documentation from referring provider
Referral NPI for receiving provider listed as 'No NPI Available' on the Elica referral form
Visits Scheduled listed as 0 on referral information page, though scheduling section shows one completed visit with Dr. Hasday — possible data discrepancy worth confirming
Second fax cover sheet (page 2 of 15) references 'corresponding medical records for authorization' but the subject line and company name fields are partially illegible

evidence: Diagnosis: S02.2XXG (ICD-10-CM) - Fracture of nasal bones, subsequent encounter for fracture with delayed healing. Referral note states: 'The patient is a 35 yo F who has a history of nasal fracture due to blunt trauma injury. Needs urgent referral to ENT.' Triage note acknowledges: 'Well past window for closed reduction.' CT confirmed right nasal bone fracture on 09/15/2025. Fracture occurred approximately 2 months before referral submission on 12/12/2025, placing it significantly outside the standard 1-2 week closed reduction window — flagged because delayed healing designation (ICD suffix 'G') and the confirmed fracture with potential need for surgical intervention (open reduction/septoplasty) require priority ENT evaluation to determine operative candidacy.

claude_vision.summary: This is an urgent referral from Dr. Nancy Ekeke (Elica Health Centers) to Sacramento ENT for a 35-year-old female with a right nasal bone fracture with delayed healing (ICD-10: S02.2XXG) sustained approximately 2 months ago from domestic assault (stepfather). CT sinus/facial from 09/15/2025 confirms the fracture without significant displacement and partial anterior ethmoid opacification; the report is included in the fax. The triage team noted the patient is 'well past the window for closed reduction,' making this a surgical consultation to assess need for open reduction or septoplasty. One completed new patient visit with Dr. Steven Hasday is documented in the scheduling system.
